52.reason: [Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since xp indices are 0 to n-1. Also, loop condition i >= 0 causes access to xp[n] and potentially xp[-1] after decrement, leading to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since xp indices are 0 to n-1. Also, loop condition i >= 0 causes access to xp[n] and potentially xp[-1] after decrement, leading to undefined behavior.]

56.reason: [When i == n, xp[i] is invalid (out-of-bounds). Even after fixing start index, logic for merging like terms is flawed: it only merges backward from current i without properly skipping merged terms or handling forward duplicates.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When i == n, xp[i] is invalid (out-of-bounds). Even after fixing start index, logic for merging like terms is flawed: it only merges backward from current i without properly skipping merged terms or handling forward duplicates.]

66.reason: [This modifies loop variable inside body in a way that interacts poorly with the for-loop's own decrement, causing skipped indices or infinite loops. Combined with starting at i=n, this leads to severe indexing errors.]
